structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband State Control Sequence
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Deadband, set primary airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint, series fan airflow setpoint to OA-min, and heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When the Zone State Is Deadband
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint. Series fan airflow setpoint = OA-min. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. The primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
b. The series fan airflow setpoint  shall be equal to OA -min.
c. Heating coil is OFF.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating State Control Sequence
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Heating, use heating loop output 0-50% to reset discharge temperature setpoint from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space setpoint with primary airflow at minimum and fan at OA-min; from 50-100% reset series fan airflow from OA-min to Sfan-htgmax while primary remains at minimum; modulate heating coil to maintain discharge temperature setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: From 0% to 50% heating loop: reset DAT setpoint from current AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space setpoint, primary airflow at minimum endpoint, fan airflow at OA-min. From 50% to 100%: reset fan airflow from OA-min to Sfan-htgmax, primary airflow at minimum endpoint. Heating coil modulated to maintain DAT at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. From 0 % to 50%, the Heating Loop  output shall reset the discharge temperature setpoint  from the current AHU SAT setpoint  to a maximum of Max∆T above space temperature setpoint . The primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint , and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be OA -min.
b. From 50 % to 100%, the Heating Loop  output shall reset the series fan airflow setpoint  from OA-min to a Sfan -htgmax. The active primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
c. The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain th e discharge temperature at setpoint . (Directly controlling heating off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Logic Design Guidance
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The heating logic leads with supply air temperature increase before fan speed increase, assuming the plenum air temperature may be below space temperature. If designer is confident the plenum is always warmer, the logic can be reversed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: In the heating Zone State , the logic keeps the fan airflow rate low while supply air temperature is increased as the first heating stage. This presumes that the temperature of the air the fan is supplying is neutral or below the space temperature, as it wo uld be if the fan draws air directly from the space , and as it might be if the fan draws air from a return air plenum that is cooled by roof and wall heat losses. In the past, return air plenums were warmed by recessed light fixtures, but pendent  lights ar e increasingly  common , so the potential for free heating from the plenum is smaller than it was. Because  there is the potential that the plenum is colder than the space due to envelope loads, the logic leads with the supply air temperature rather than with an increase in fan speed. If the designer is confident that the plenum will always be warmer, the logic  can be reversed.
